original_hypothesis.title: Unnecessary Unlimited Asset Approval to Liquidation Pair
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The vault grants the liquidation pair unlimited approval to spend its underlying asset, exposing assets if the LP is compromised.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The liquidation flow only requires the LP to receive vault shares and send prize tokens. It never needs to pull underlying assets from the vault. Granting `type(uint256).max` approval violates the principle of least privilege.
preconditions: Liquidation pair contract is compromised or malicious

analysis_questions: Does the LP ever actually need this approval?
What is the impact if the LP calls `transferFrom`?
validation_requirements: Verify LP implementation does not require asset approval

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A malicious LP contract is set by a compromised owner. The LP calls `asset.transferFrom(vault, attacker, balance)` to steal all underlying assets.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Theft of all underlying assets in the vault

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing security review of LP integration requirements

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Any time after LP is set

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
